overview: The XLIFF Filter processes **XLIFF 1.2** (XML Localisation Interchange File Format) documents, the OASIS standard for transporting translatable text between translation tools. It supports standard XLIFF as well as vendor-specific variants including **SDLXLIFF** and **IWS XLIFF** (Idiom WorldServer) through pre-defined configurations. The filter handles segmentation via `<seg-source>`, `<alt-trans>` elements for translation matches, ITS annotations, and maps XLIFF attributes to Okapi resource properties.

extraction.preserveSpaceByDefault.help:
Controls whether whitespace in `<source>` and `<target>` content is preserved or unwrapped by default.

Normally, the filter checks each `<trans-unit>` for `xml:space="preserve"`. This option sets the default behavior when `xml:space` is **not** present — if enabled, whitespace is preserved; if disabled, content is unwrapped.

extraction.includeExtensions.help: Controls whether non-standard XLIFF extension elements are included in the extracted resources. Extension elements are vendor-specific additions outside the core XLIFF namespace.

extraction.includeIts.help: Controls whether ITS (Internationalization Tag Set) and ITSXLF annotations are included in the extracted resources.

extraction.inlineCdata.help: Treats CDATA sections found within translatable content as inline codes rather than separate content blocks.

extraction.subAsTextUnit.help: Processes `<sub>` elements as independent text units rather than including them as part of their parent inline code.
notes: The content of `<sub>` elements is currently not fully supported as text. Any element found inside `<bpt>`, `<ept>`, `<ph>`, and `<it>` (including `<sub>`) is included in the code of the parent inline element. A warning is generated when a `<sub>` element is detected.

extraction.ignoreInputSegmentation.help:
Strips all segmentation information from the input XLIFF. All segmented content is reduced to unsegmented content during extraction.

**Warning:** Any `<alt-trans>` data attached to a specific segment is also lost when this option is enabled.

extraction.skipNoMrkSegSource.help: Skips `<seg-source>` elements that do not contain explicit `<mrk mtype='seg'>` segment markers. Normally, a `<seg-source>` without markers is treated as a single segment encompassing all content.

extraction.fallbackToID.help: Uses the `id` attribute of the `<trans-unit>` element as the text unit name when `resname` is not present. Useful for XLIFF documents that use `resname`-like values for `id` but omit the `resname` attribute.

extraction.forceUniqueIds.help: Ensures all text unit IDs are unique in the extracted output. If duplicate IDs are found, they are made unique by appending a disambiguating suffix.

states.targetStateMode.help: Controls how the `state` attribute on `<target>` elements is handled in the output.
states.targetStateMode.values:
Integer value selecting the state handling mode:
- `0` — Do not modify the state attribute
- Other values select different state update strategies

states.targetStateValue.help: The value to set for the `state` attribute on `<target>` elements when state modification is active.

states.useTranslationTargetState.help: Uses translation-specific target state handling when processing translated segments.

states.editAltTrans.help: Allows existing `<alt-trans>` elements in the input to be modified in the output. Existing entries are treated like newly added ones.

states.addAltTrans.help: Allows new `<alt-trans>` elements to be added to the output. For example, when the Leveraging Step is applied, translation matches found during leveraging are included as `<alt-trans>` entries.

states.addAltTransGMode.help:
Controls the notation used for inline codes in newly added `<alt-trans>` elements.

- **Enabled**: Uses `<g>`/`<x>` notation
- **Disabled**: Uses `<bpt>`/`<ept>`/`<ph>` notation

output.addTargetLanguage.help: Adds the `target-language` attribute to `<file>` elements if it is not already present in the input document.

output.overrideTargetLanguage.help:
Overrides the target language in the output document. Sets the `target-language` attribute and `xml:lang` on all `<target>` elements to the user-specified target language, regardless of original values.

Useful when using an XLIFF document as a template for multiple target languages.
notes: This option may result in outputs where existing `<alt-trans>` elements no longer correspond to the target language of a given `<trans-unit>`.

output.allowEmptyTargets.help: Prevents the filter from copying source text into empty `<target>` elements. By default, if a target is empty, the source content is used as a fallback.

output.alwaysAddTargets.help: Ensures `<target>` elements are always present in the output, even for trans-units that did not have them in the input.

output.alwaysUseSegSource.help: Forces all text units in the output to use `<seg-source>` representation, even if the original input was not segmented.

output.outputSegmentationType.help: Controls how segmentation is represented in the output XLIFF document.
output.outputSegmentationType.values:
One of the following segmentation strategies:
- **Segment only if input is segmented** — Preserves the input representation; only uses `<seg-source>` where the input already had it
- **Always segment** — All text units use `<seg-source>`, even single-segment entries
- **Never segment** — Removes all `<seg-source>` elements from output
- **Segment only if multi-part** — Only text units with more than one part get `<seg-source>`, regardless of input

subfilters.cdataSubfilter.help: Specifies a sub-filter configuration ID to process CDATA section content. The content within CDATA sections will be passed through the specified filter for further extraction.
subfilters.cdataSubfilter.values: An Okapi filter configuration ID (e.g. `okf_html`), or empty string for no sub-filtering

subfilters.pcdataSubfilter.help: Specifies a sub-filter configuration ID to process PCDATA (parsed character data) content. The text content will be passed through the specified filter for further extraction.
subfilters.pcdataSubfilter.values: An Okapi filter configuration ID (e.g. `okf_html`), or empty string for no sub-filtering

parser.useCustomParser.help: Uses an XML stream parser different from the JVM default. The default custom parser is **Woodstox**, which avoids a stack overflow bug in the standard Java XML parser when processing XLIFF documents with large chunks of element content (common in SDLXLIFF files).
notes: The Java parser that comes with most VMs implements whitespace character collapsing using a recursive method that can cause a **stack overflow error** in XLIFF documents with large element content (e.g. SDLXLIFF files). The Woodstox parser avoids this issue.

parser.factoryClass.help: The fully-qualified Java class name for the XML stream parser factory. Only used when the custom parser option is enabled.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this value to be used

sdl.useSdlXliffWriter.help: Enables the SDL-specific XLIFF writer for proper handling of SDLXLIFF files. Required for correct output when processing `.sdlxliff` documents.

sdl.useSegsForSdlProps.help: Stores SDL properties (`locked`, `conf`, `origin`) at the segment level rather than only on the TextContainer of the target.
notes: SDL properties `locked`, `conf`, and `origin` are stored in the TextContainer of the target rather than in each segment. If there are several segments, the values are those of the last segment. From M35 on, the three properties are also stored at the segment level with correct values, but those segment-level properties are **read-only**.

sdl.sdlSegConfValue.help: Sets the SDL segment confirmation level value to apply to segments in the output.
sdl.sdlSegConfValue.values: SDL confirmation values: `Draft`, `Translated`, `RejectedTranslation`, `ApprovedTranslation`, `RejectedSignOff`, `ApprovedSignOff`, or empty string to leave unchanged

sdl.sdlSegLockedValue.help: Sets the locked status for SDL segments in the output.
sdl.sdlSegLockedValue.values: Empty string to leave unchanged, or a locked status value

sdl.sdlSegOriginValue.help: Sets the origin value for SDL segments in the output (e.g. the source of the translation).
sdl.sdlSegOriginValue.values: An origin identifier string (e.g. `interactive`, `tm`, `mt`), or empty string to leave unchanged

iws.useIwsXliffWriter.help: Enables the Idiom WorldServer-specific XLIFF writer for proper handling of IWS XLIFF files.

iws.iwsBlockFinished.help: Blocks (skips) trans-units that have the IWS `finished` status. When enabled, already-finished segments are excluded from processing.

iws.iwsBlockLockStatus.help: Blocks (skips) trans-units that are locked in IWS.

iws.iwsBlockMultipleExact.help: Blocks (skips) trans-units that have multiple exact matches in IWS.

iws.iwsBlockTmScore.help: Blocks (skips) trans-units based on their IWS TM score threshold.

dependsOn.condition: specifies the score threshold when this is enabled

iws.iwsBlockTmScoreValue.help: The TM score threshold value for blocking trans-units. Trans-units with a TM score at or above this value are blocked.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this value to take effect

iws.iwsIncludeMultipleExact.help: Includes trans-units with multiple exact matches from IWS, rather than excluding them.

iws.iwsRemoveTmOrigin.help: Removes the TM origin metadata from IWS trans-units in the output.

iws.iwsTransStatusValue.help: Sets the IWS translation status value on output trans-units.

iws.iwsTransTypeValue.help: Sets the IWS translation type value on output trans-units.

limitations: The content of `<sub>` elements is not fully supported as text. Elements inside `<bpt>`, `<ept>`, `<ph>`, and `<it>` (including `<sub>`) are included in the code of the parent inline element. A warning is generated when `<sub>` is detected.

The `<mrk mtype='protected'>` marker converts content into an inline code. If `<mrk mtype='x-its-translate-yes'>` is nested within it, it is not supported and its content is placed into the original data part of the inline code.

For SDLXLIFF: SDL properties `locked`, `conf`, and `origin` are stored at the TextContainer level (not per-segment). With multiple segments, values come from the last segment. From M35+, segment-level properties exist but are **read-only**.
processingNotes: **Encoding detection**: Uses the document's encoding declaration if present, otherwise defaults to UTF-8 regardless of the specified default encoding.
**BOM handling**: Output includes a BOM only if the input was UTF-8 and had a BOM. Transcoding from non-UTF-8 to UTF-8 never adds a BOM.
**Line breaks**: Output preserves the same line-break style as the input document.
**Whitespace**: Content in `<source>`/`<target>` is unwrapped unless `xml:space="preserve"` is set on the `<trans-unit>`.
**seg-source validation**: If the content of `<seg-source>` (without segment markers) does not match `<source>`, the segmentation is discarded.
**alt-trans mapping**: Only `<alt-trans>` elements with `alttranstype="proposal"` (or no `alttranstype`) are mapped to `AltTranslationsAnnotation`. Entries are sorted by match type and score.
**Match scoring**: `match-quality` is used as score if it's an integer or percentage; otherwise score defaults to 0. Entries with score >99 are EXACT matches, >0 are FUZZY.

**maxbytes mapping**: The `maxbytes` attribute maps to `its-storageSize`. Uses `its:storageSizeEncoding` if present, otherwise defaults to UTF-8. Note that `its:storageSize` is **not** recognized — size must be declared via `maxbytes`.

examples.title: Standard XLIFF 1.2 processing
examples.description: Basic XLIFF document structure showing how elements map to Okapi resources. The `resname` becomes the text unit name, `approved` maps to the target's approved property, and `<note>` elements map based on their `annotates` attribute.
examples.input:
```xml
<xliff version="1.2" xmlns="urn:oasis:names:tc:xliff:document:1.2">
  <file source-language="en" target-language="fr" datatype="plaintext">
    <body>
      <trans-unit id="1" resname="greeting" approved="yes">
        <source>Hello</source>
        <target>Bonjour</target>
        <note annotates="source">Informal greeting</note>
      </trans-unit>
    </body>
  </file>
</xliff>
```

examples.title: SDLXLIFF configuration
examples.description: Use the `okf_xliff-sdl` pre-defined configuration for SDLXLIFF files. This enables the SDL-specific writer and segment property handling.
examples.config:
```yaml
sdl:
  useSdlXliffWriter: true
  useSegsForSdlProps: true
parser:
  useCustomParser: true
  factoryClass: com.ctc.wstx.stax.WstxInputFactory
```

examples.title: Template-based multilingual output
examples.description: Using an XLIFF as a template for multiple target languages. Enable `overrideTargetLanguage` so the output's `target-language` and all `xml:lang` attributes on `<target>` elements are set to the pipeline's target language.
